title: Dell VxRail Secure Remote Services / Support Connectivity Degradation
exclusions: Air-gapped or export-controlled environments where contractually no Dell support tunnel may exist, and where legal policy forbids host-side phone-home: this UC’s thresholds do not apply; use an offline RMA process instead. Clusters with Dell-managed APEX operations where another team owns the SCG: align RACI before duplicating pages. pure VMware estates without VxRail Manager—no `/rest/vxm/v1/support/network` contract; do not point this search at a generic vCenter. Customers still on a deprecated-only SRS design that Dell has scheduled for retirement—monitor logs aggressively but expect field drift; prefer SCG migration projects. Development clusters where support is intentionally disabled: exclude via baseline or a dedicated `index=vxrail_labs`. Network-only UCs (WAN RTT) without gateway logs— you will lack cross-validation; either add the UF path or accept higher ambiguity. HPE, Cisco, or Nutanix HCI—out of scope. Array-only telemetry (PowerStore, PowerMax) without VxM—wrong control plane. UC-19.1.48 vSAN resync: different failure mode; pair, do not substitute.
evidence: Saved search and dashboard on `index=vxrail` with `vxrail:vxm:support_status` plus optional `dell:srs:console_log` correlation, scoped by `site_id` for auditor review. Export last-90-day run history for support-channel availability reviews.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A lab or DR cluster where SCG is shut down for more than 60 minutes: `esrs_status` in API JSON should read DISCONNECTED or heartbeat age should exceed 60 when translated to minutes, and the saved search should classify `critical` for that cluster with `in_planned=0`, optionally echoed by `dell:srs:console_log` substrings in the last hour if SRS is still in path.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A planned `vxrail_change_windows` row with `start_utc` and `end_utc` that bracket the poll `poll_time` for that `cluster_id` should set `in_planned=1` and downgrade the operator-visible severity to `info` so the change bridge is not paged for an approved maintenance.

dataSources: `index=vxrail` with `sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:support_status"` (HEC from scripted `GET /rest/vxm/v1/support/network` on VxRail Manager), optional `dell:scg:health` (SCG REST on port 5700 if you add a poller), optional `dell:srs:console_log` and `dell:srs:diagnostic_log` (UF `monitor` of `/var/log/EMC/SRSGateway/`), and optional `vxrail:vxm:log` (UF of `/var/log/vmware/vxrail/*.log` for free-text token correlation). There is no Splunkbase TA that ships these sourcetypes; names are your deployment contract. Real API field names: `esrs_status`, `last_heartbeat_utc`, `last_heartbeat_age_seconds`, `last_phone_home_utc`, `last_phone_home_age_seconds`, `tls_certificate_status`, `tls_certificate_expires_utc`, `outbound_proxy_enabled`, `esrs_gateway_address`, `site_id`, `service_tag`, `scg_mode`.
app: Custom `TA_vxrail_support` (Python to HEC) plus optional UF; optional Splunk Connect for Syslog for gateway logs. No `Splunk Add-on for Dell VxRail` on Splunkbase—build and own the TA id.
spl:
// UC-19.1.49 — Dell VxRail Secure Remote Services / Support Connectivity Degradation.
// Polls VxRail Manager `/rest/vxm/v1/support/network` (sourcetype `vxrail:vxm:support_status`)
// and (optionally) the SCG appliance for ESRS heartbeat / phone-home age, TLS material status,
// and reachability. Tiers severity by hours since last SCG heartbeat, phone-home gap vs vendor SLA,
// and TLS-cert validity. Suppresses operator-driven SCG maintenance windows via lookup.
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:support_status" earliest=-2h
| eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, site_id, "unknown")
| eval site_id=coalesce(site_id, cluster_id, "")
| eval service_tag=coalesce(service_tag, "")
| eval esrs_status=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(esrs_status, srs_status, connection_status, ""))))
| eval hb_raw=tonumber(last_heartbeat_age_seconds, 10)
| eval ph_raw=tonumber(last_phone_home_age_seconds, 10)
| eval hb_utc=coalesce(last_heartbeat_utc, "")
| eval ph_utc=coalesce(last_phone_home_utc, "")
| eval hb_utc_t=coalesce( strptime(hb_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), strptime(hb_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") )
| eval ph_utc_t=coalesce( strptime(ph_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), strptime(ph_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") )
| eval hb_sec=if(isnotnull(hb_raw) AND hb_raw>=0, hb_raw, if(isnotnull(hb_utc_t), now()-hb_utc_t, null()))
| eval ph_sec=if(isnotnull(ph_raw) AND ph_raw>=0, ph_raw, if(isnotnull(ph_utc_t), now()-ph_utc_t, null()))
| eval heartbeat_age_min=if(isnotnull(hb_sec), round(hb_sec/60, 1), null())
| eval phone_home_age_hr=if(isnotnull(ph_sec), round(ph_sec/3600, 1), null())
| eval tls_certificate_status=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(tls_certificate_status, cert_status, "UNKNOWN"))))
| eval cert_ex_t=coalesce( strptime(tls_certificate_expires_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), strptime(tls_certificate_expires_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") )
| eval cert_expires_days=if(isnotnull(cert_ex_t), round((cert_ex_t-now())/86400, 1), null())
| lookup vxrail_support_baseline cluster_id OUTPUT expected_heartbeat_window_min, expected_phone_home_window_hr, business_service, on_call_team, runbook_url, dell_account_team_contact, allow_offline_window_pattern
| eval exp_hb_min=tonumber(expected_heartbeat_window_min, 10)
| eval exp_hb_min=if(isnotnull(exp_hb_min) AND exp_hb_min>0, exp_hb_min, 20)
| eval exp_ph_hr=tonumber(expected_phone_home_window_hr, 10)
| eval exp_ph_hr=if(isnotnull(exp_ph_hr) AND exp_ph_hr>0, exp_ph_hr, 30)
| stats
    max(_time) as poll_time
    latest(cluster_id) as cluster_id
    latest(site_id) as site_id
    latest(service_tag) as service_tag
    latest(esrs_status) as esrs_status
    latest(heartbeat_age_min) as heartbeat_age_min
    latest(phone_home_age_hr) as phone_home_age_hr
    latest(tls_certificate_status) as tls_certificate_status
    latest(cert_expires_days) as cert_expires_days
    latest(business_service) as business_service
    latest(on_call_team) as on_call_team
    latest(runbook_url) as runbook_url
    latest(dell_account_team_contact) as dell_account_team_contact
    latest(outbound_proxy_enabled) as outbound_proxy_enabled
    latest(exp_hb_min) as exp_hb_min
    latest(exp_ph_hr) as exp_ph_hr
  by cluster_id
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| inputlookup vxrail_change_windows.csv
     | eval cw_start=strptime(start_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")
     | eval cw_start=coalesce(cw_start, strptime(start_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
     | eval cw_end=strptime(end_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")
     | eval cw_end=coalesce(cw_end, strptime(end_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
     | fields cluster_id, cw_start, cw_end, scope_filter, ticket_id ]
| eval in_planned=if(isnotnull(cw_start) AND isnotnull(cw_end) AND poll_time>=cw_start AND poll_time<=cw_end, 1, 0)
| stats
    max(in_planned) as in_planned
    values(ticket_id) as change_tickets
    first(poll_time) as poll_time
    first(cluster_id) as cluster_id
    first(site_id) as site_id
    first(service_tag) as service_tag
    first(esrs_status) as esrs_status
    first(heartbeat_age_min) as heartbeat_age_min
    first(phone_home_age_hr) as phone_home_age_hr
    first(tls_certificate_status) as tls_certificate_status
    first(cert_expires_days) as cert_expires_days
    first(business_service) as business_service
    first(on_call_team) as on_call_team
    first(runbook_url) as runbook_url
    first(dell_account_team_contact) as dell_account_team_contact
    first(outbound_proxy_enabled) as outbound_proxy_enabled
    first(exp_hb_min) as exp_hb_min
    first(exp_ph_hr) as exp_ph_hr
  by cluster_id
| appendcols [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="dell:srs:console_log" earliest=-1h (HeartbeatFailed OR "ConnectivityFailure" OR "AuthenticationFailed")
  | stats count as srs_log_error_hits
  | fillnull value=0 srs_log_error_hits ]
| eval recommended_action=case(
    in_planned=1, "Planned work window: confirm change ticket; no page unless condition persists after cw_end",
    (esrs_status="DISCONNECTED" OR esrs_status="UNKNOWN") AND in_planned=0 AND (isnull(heartbeat_age_min) OR heartbeat_age_min>60), "P1: verify SCG/SRS VM, routing, DNS, and VxM to gateway 9443 path; if still down engage Dell TSE; note RMA speed impact vs UC-19.1.47",
    tls_certificate_status="EXPIRED" OR (isnotnull(cert_expires_days) AND cert_expires_days<7), "Renew or fix TLS trust for SCG/VxM/enterprise CA before the tunnel is rejected",
    (isnotnull(heartbeat_age_min) AND heartbeat_age_min>exp_hb_min AND heartbeat_age_min<=60) OR (isnotnull(phone_home_age_hr) AND phone_home_age_hr>exp_ph_hr), "Degraded: if outbound_proxy_enabled=1 validate proxy user/password; verify SCG services; compare fields to VxM SupportConnectivity UI",
    tls_certificate_status="EXPIRING_SOON" OR (isnotnull(cert_expires_days) AND cert_expires_days<30), "Schedule certificate replacement before support channel is impacted",
    1=1, "Triage with runbook_url; verify HEC and poller if key fields are null")
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    in_planned=1, "info",
    tls_certificate_status="EXPIRED" OR (isnotnull(cert_expires_days) AND cert_expires_days<7), "critical",
    (esrs_status="DISCONNECTED" OR esrs_status="UNKNOWN") AND in_planned=0 AND (isnull(heartbeat_age_min) OR heartbeat_age_min>60), "critical",
    (isnotnull(heartbeat_age_min) AND heartbeat_age_min>exp_hb_min AND heartbeat_age_min<=60) OR (isnotnull(phone_home_age_hr) AND phone_home_age_hr>exp_ph_hr), "warning",
    tls_certificate_status="EXPIRING_SOON" OR (isnotnull(cert_expires_days) AND cert_expires_days<30), "warning",
    esrs_status="CONNECTED" AND isnotnull(heartbeat_age_min) AND heartbeat_age_min<=exp_hb_min, "compliant",
    1=1, "info")
| eval recommended_action=if(srs_log_error_hits>0, recommended_action + " (SRS console log errors in last 1h)", recommended_action)
| table cluster_id, site_id, service_tag, esrs_status, heartbeat_age_min, phone_home_age_hr, tls_certificate_status, cert_expires_days, severity_bucket, business_service, recommended_action, runbook_url, dell_account_team_contact, in_planned, change_tickets, srs_log_error_hits, outbound_proxy_enabled
description: VxRail proactive support and automated log collection depend on outbound connectivity through Dell Secure Connect Gateway (SCG) or, in legacy sites, the SRS Gateway path into Dell support systems. A silent or gradual failure delays Dell’s visibility into hardware faults (including predictive physical-disk signals correlated in UC-19.1.47), slows or blocks automated case creation and log bundles, and weakens the evidence chain for ProSupport+ contract SLAs that assume an intact phone-home and heartbeat cadence. This use case is the support-tunnel and telemetry path—not vSAN object health (UC-19.1.48) and not per-drive SMART (UC-19.1.47).
value: Restores vendor telemetry paths so predictive disk and node events still reach Dell support without manual bundle uploads, keeps SCG heartbeats and phone-home within your contracted windows, and gives operations an early, cluster-level signal when trust material (TLS) or network reachability to the gateway is degrading before RMA and severity-one bridges compress into the same day.
implementation: Create `index=vxrail` and HEC. Deploy `TA_vxrail_support` on a Heavy Forwarder with `script:///opt/splunk/etc/apps/TA_vxrail_support/bin/vxm_support_poller.py` on 900s against `/rest/vxm/v1/support/network`. Add UF `monitor` for SRS/SCG logs. Install `lookups/vxrail_support_baseline.csv` and `lookups/vxrail_change_windows.csv` with a `cluster_id` column to join planned outages. CIM: tag normalized vendor alerts into the Alerts data model; map session-style gateway telemetry to Network_Sessions when you align fields in a child TA. Schedule the `spl` every 30m over -2h, alert on `severity_bucket` in (critical, warning) for production, suppress 4h on `cluster_id`, and set drilldown to your VxM support connectivity page.
visualization: Single value: count of `severity_bucket` critical in window. Table: `cluster_id`, `site_id`, `esrs_status`, `heartbeat_age_min`, `phone_home_age_hr`, `tls_certificate_status`, `cert_expires_days`, `severity_bucket`, `in_planned`, `business_service`, `runbook_url`, `dell_account_team_contact`, `srs_log_error_hits`. Timechart: `heartbeat_age_min` by cluster. Heatmap: phone-home age by `site_id`.
schema: CIM: map vendor threshold breaches to Alerts; map remote gateway session metadata to Network_Sessions when fieldalias completes.

references.title: Dell — Secure Connect Gateway (SCG) product documentation
NIST Special Publication 800-53 Rev. 5 (SI-4, CA-7 context)

knownFalsePositives: Planned VxRail LCM or SCG vApp upgrade windows that restart the gateway and bounce HTTPS paths—suppress with vxrail_change_windows rows per cluster, not a global mute. Monthly SCG OS patching and quarterly firmware waves that show DISCONNECTED for under your documented maximum—extend expected_heartbeat_window_min in vxrail_support_baseline after you measure your real P95 bounce time. Intentional change-freeze or “support offline” approved projects where the business turns off phone-home: use allow_offline_window_pattern in the baseline and an executive ticket, or expect a deliberate info-tier row. Global TLS or enterprise PKI re-issuance for the SCG or proxy certificate where VxM briefly reports EXPIRING_SOON or UNKNOWN while intermediates re-chain—treat as warning, not P1, if the customer PKI runbook is active. vSAN or ESXi work that is unrelated to support tunnels but co-incident with a bad poll—dedupe with VxM UI before paging. Differences between primary and secondary SCG when only one path is cycled in dual-SCG—may look like a flap until both are up; do not re-use UC-19.1.48’s resync heuristics here.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• `index=vxrail` (create in Splunk) and a non-default HEC token whose allowed indexes include vxrail; Heavy Forwarder or an automation host that can reach `https://<vxm-fqdn>/rest/vxm/v1/support/network` on TCP 443.
• A VxRail Manager local or SSO user with read access to the support network API; credentials outside version control, pulled at runtime (password vault, 0600 file, or Splunk external credential store pattern your security team approves).
• For legacy SRS: a Universal Forwarder or rsyslog path that can read `/var/log/EMC/SRSGateway/srs_console.log` and `srs_diagnostic.log` on the gateway VM; for SCG, decide whether you also poll `https://<scg-fqdn>:5700/api/v1/health` into `dell:scg:health` as a second opinion (optional).
• NTP: VxM, collection host, and gateway clocks aligned so `last_heartbeat_utc` and `_time` compare fairly in validation.
• Regulatory mapping note: this operational detector supports evidence for NIST 800-53 Rev.5 SI-4 (system monitoring) and CA-7 (continuous monitoring) by proving you continuously evaluate vendor support-channel availability—not by replacing your SIEM, but by closing the loop on a control plane that many audits forget until an RMA stalls.

**Step 1 — Identify (why this is not "just another network ping")**
Dell’s ability to pre-emptively replace a failing physical disk (correlated with drive telemetry in UC-19.1.47) and to auto-open cases depends on SCG (Secure Connect Gateway) or legacy SRS shipping heartbeats, phone-home log bundles, and event-triggered uploads to CloudIQ. SCG’s default ~5 minute heartbeat and scheduled auto-logs (often 12–24h) mean a gap over ~15 minutes is already degraded, and a gap over ~60 minutes is often service-affecting for automatic Dell-side workflows. When the tunnel is down, a predictive disk may still be visible in-cluster, but Dell may not auto-create the case or RMA, and a customer may fall back to manual log bundles—adding days to a same-day part story. ProSupport+ and similar programs often assume connectivity exists for entitlement enforcement; a silent break shows up in SLAs and executive reviews only after a missed replacement window. Dell’s transition from the legacy SRS Gateway product toward SCG (field deployments still mix both) means you must know which path each site uses and not mis-label SCG as "SRS" in runbooks. This UC is intentionally the third VxRail axis: (1) UC-19.1.47 disks, (2) UC-19.1.48 vSAN object/resync, (3) this file—support connective tissue to Dell. It does not replace network reachability tests for user VLANs, nor does it replace vCenter health; it targets the VxM `/support/network` contract and the gateway logs that prove the same story. Governance teams should file this saved search and dashboard in the same compliance pack as your phone-home and secure-remote-access policies.

**Step 2 — Implement (data collection, conf files, lookups)**
(1) Python scripted input — place `TA_vxrail_support` on the Heavy Forwarder. Example: `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_vxrail_support/bin/vxm_support_poller.py` (mode 0755) reads `/etc/splunk-vxrail-creds.cnf` (0600) with `VXRM_FQDN`, `VXRM_USER`, `VXRM_PASS` (or token flow if you wrap SSO; Basic is the common first integration), `HEC_URL`, `HEC_TOKEN`, optional `VXRM_CA_BUNDLE` for enterprise CA validation. The script: `GET https://$VXRM_FQDN/rest/vxm/v1/support/network` with `Accept: application/json`, Basic auth or your approved header mode, `verify=` pointed at your CA bundle, timeout 120s, parse JSON, inject `cluster_id` and `ingest_label` for multi-cluster pollers, `POST` one HEC event per poll with time set from script clock or JSON `last_heartbeat_utc` (prefer receipt time in pilot; align `_time` policy in `props`). Self-signed VxM certs: either import your lab root into `verify=`, or temporary lab-only `SSL_NO_VERIFY=1` in a private fork—document that production must terminate verification properly.

`inputs.conf` (Heavy Forwarder):
```ini
[script:///opt/splunk/etc/apps/TA_vxrail_support/bin/vxm_support_poller.py]
interval = 900
sourcetype = vxrail:vxm:support_status
source = vxm:support:network
index = vxrail
disabled = 0
```

`props.conf` for JSON API snapshot (tune `TIME_*` only if you rely on raw JSON for `_time` instead of HEC clock):
```ini
[vxrail:vxm:support_status]
TRUNCATE = 100000
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
KV_MODE = none
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TZ = UTC
# If extracting event time from embedded ISO with Z:
# TIME_PREFIX = last_heartbeat_utc"\s*:\s*"
# TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ
# For HEC-envelope time (recommended first): omit TIME_PREFIX and let the HEC receiver stamp _time.
```

(Notes: in production many teams set `_time` from the poller’s `time` in the HEC envelope and use INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS only for search fields, not for `_time`—pick one and document. TZ should be UTC for `last_heartbeat_utc` comparisons.)

(2) Universal Forwarder on SRS (legacy) VM — `inputs.conf`:
```ini
[monitor:///var/log/EMC/SRSGateway/srs_console.log]
index = vxrail
sourcetype = dell:srs:console_log
host = your-srs-fqdn
disabled = 0

[monitor:///var/log/EMC/SRSGateway/srs_diagnostic.log]
index = vxrail
sourcetype = dell:srs:diagnostic_log
host = your-srs-fqdn
disabled = 0
```

If you deploy a SCG vApp, align paths with the vendor’s current log directory (some sites mirror under `/var/log/scg`); adjust `sourcetype` to your naming standard. Optional SCG REST poller: `https://<scg>:5700/api/v1/health` → `dell:scg:health` with its own `props` JSON stanza.

`props.conf` for `dell:srs:console_log`:
```ini
[dell:srs:console_log]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
TRUNCATE = 20000
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 40
# Example extractions; tune against _raw
EXTRACT-srs_sev = ^(?<timestamp>\S+\s+\S+)\s+(?<component>\S+)\s+(?<severity>\S+)\s+(?<message>.+)
```

(3) Optional: `vxrail:vxm:log` monitor for `/var/log/vmware/vxrail/vxrail-mgr.log` and `gw.log` to grep tokens like SRS, SCG, ConnectivityFailure, HeartbeatFailed when API polling is not yet deployed.

(4) Lookups — `lookups/vxrail_support_baseline.csv` (on Search Head) example rows:
```
cluster_id,site_id,expected_heartbeat_window_min,expected_phone_home_window_hr,business_service,on_call_team,runbook_url,dell_account_team_contact,allow_offline_window_pattern
VXR-PROD-01,DELL-10001-A,20,30,vSphere ERP,unix-oncall,https://wiki.example/vxrail-srs,accounteam@dell.com,
VXR-DR-22,DELL-10002-B,20,36,vSphere DR,dr-bridge,https://wiki.example/vxrail-srs,other.team@dell.com,none
VXR-LAB-99,DELL-9-LAB,45,72,Test only,lab,https://wiki.example/vxrail-srs,none,weekends
```

(5) `lookups/vxrail_change_windows.csv` — one row per maintenance window, keyed by `cluster_id` to match the `join` in the SPL. Example:
```
cluster_id,start_utc,end_utc,scope_filter,ticket_id
VXR-PROD-01,2026-05-10T20:00:00Z,2026-05-10T23:30:00Z,*,CHG-7788
VXR-DR-22,2026-05-11T00:00:00Z,2026-05-11T04:00:00Z,*,CHG-7789
```

(6) `transforms.conf` and `collections.conf` if you move these to KV Store later; for crawl/walk, CSV on the search head is enough.

(7) Optional CloudIQ — if the enterprise has CloudIQ API access, add a second poller and merge at dashboard level, not in the same sourcetype without schema planning.

(8) Minimal `vxm_support_poller.py` (illustrative; add logging, retry/backoff, and a secrets back-end for production):

```python
#!/opt/splunk/bin/python3
import base64, json, os, ssl, sys, time, urllib.error, urllib.request
def read_cnf(path):
    o = {}
    with open(path) as f:
        for line in f:
            s = line.strip()
            if not s or s.startswith("#") or "=" not in s:
                continue
            k, v = s.split("=", 1)
            o[k.strip()] = v.strip()
    return o
def hec_post(url, token, payload, src):
    u = url.rstrip("/")
    if not u.endswith("event"):
        u = u + "/services/collector/event"
    body = json.dumps(
        {
            "time": int(time.time()),
            "sourcetype": "vxrail:vxm:support_status",
            "source": src,
            "index": "vxrail",
            "event": payload,
        }
    )
    r = urllib.request.Request(
        u,
        data=body.encode("utf-8"),
        headers={"Authorization": "Splunk " + token, "Content-Type": "application/json"},
    )
    ctx = None
    if os.environ.get("VXRM_INSECURE") == "1":
        ctx = ssl._create_unverified_context()
    with urllib.request.urlopen(r, context=ctx, timeout=120) as resp:
        _ = resp.read()
def main():
    c = read_cnf("/etc/splunk-vxrail-creds.cnf")
    vxm = "https://%s/rest/vxm/v1/support/network" % c["VXRM_FQDN"]
    b64 = base64.b64encode(("%s:%s" % (c["VXRM_USER"], c["VXRM_PASS"])).encode()).decode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(vxm, headers={"Authorization": "Basic " + b64, "Accept": "application/json"})
    ctx = None
    if os.environ.get("VXRM_INSECURE") == "1":
        ctx = ssl._create_unverified_context()
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=120) as resp:
        j = json.loads(resp.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
    if isinstance(j, dict):
        j.setdefault("cluster_id", c.get("CLUSTER_ID", ""))
    hec_post(c["HEC_URL"], c["HEC_TOKEN"], j, "vxm:%s" % c["VXRM_FQDN"])
if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--dry-run" in sys.argv:
        c = read_cnf("/etc/splunk-vxrail-creds.cnf")
        vxm = "https://%s/rest/vxm/v1/support/network" % c["VXRM_FQDN"]
        b64 = base64.b64encode(("%s:%s" % (c["VXRM_USER"], c["VXRM_PASS"])).encode()).decode()
        req = urllib.request.Request(vxm, headers={"Authorization": "Basic " + b64, "Accept": "application/json"})
        ctx = ssl._create_unverified_context() if os.environ.get("VXRM_INSECURE") == "1" else None
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=ctx, timeout=120) as resp:
            print(resp.read().decode("utf-8", "replace"))
    else:
        main()
```

(9) The SPL to save as a report/alert (must match the top-level `spl` field in this file verbatim in the next fenced block):

```spl
// UC-19.1.49 — Dell VxRail Secure Remote Services / Support Connectivity Degradation.
// Polls VxRail Manager `/rest/vxm/v1/support/network` (sourcetype `vxrail:vxm:support_status`)
// and (optionally) the SCG appliance for ESRS heartbeat / phone-home age, TLS material status,
// and reachability. Tiers severity by hours since last SCG heartbeat, phone-home gap vs vendor SLA,
// and TLS-cert validity. Suppresses operator-driven SCG maintenance windows via lookup.
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:support_status" earliest=-2h
| eval cluster_id=coalesce(cluster_id, site_id, "unknown")
| eval site_id=coalesce(site_id, cluster_id, "")
| eval service_tag=coalesce(service_tag, "")
| eval esrs_status=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(esrs_status, srs_status, connection_status, ""))))
| eval hb_raw=tonumber(last_heartbeat_age_seconds, 10)
| eval ph_raw=tonumber(last_phone_home_age_seconds, 10)
| eval hb_utc=coalesce(last_heartbeat_utc, "")
| eval ph_utc=coalesce(last_phone_home_utc, "")
| eval hb_utc_t=coalesce( strptime(hb_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), strptime(hb_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") )
| eval ph_utc_t=coalesce( strptime(ph_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), strptime(ph_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") )
| eval hb_sec=if(isnotnull(hb_raw) AND hb_raw>=0, hb_raw, if(isnotnull(hb_utc_t), now()-hb_utc_t, null()))
| eval ph_sec=if(isnotnull(ph_raw) AND ph_raw>=0, ph_raw, if(isnotnull(ph_utc_t), now()-ph_utc_t, null()))
| eval heartbeat_age_min=if(isnotnull(hb_sec), round(hb_sec/60, 1), null())
| eval phone_home_age_hr=if(isnotnull(ph_sec), round(ph_sec/3600, 1), null())
| eval tls_certificate_status=upper(trim(toString(coalesce(tls_certificate_status, cert_status, "UNKNOWN"))))
| eval cert_ex_t=coalesce( strptime(tls_certificate_expires_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ"), strptime(tls_certificate_expires_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ") )
| eval cert_expires_days=if(isnotnull(cert_ex_t), round((cert_ex_t-now())/86400, 1), null())
| lookup vxrail_support_baseline cluster_id OUTPUT expected_heartbeat_window_min, expected_phone_home_window_hr, business_service, on_call_team, runbook_url, dell_account_team_contact, allow_offline_window_pattern
| eval exp_hb_min=tonumber(expected_heartbeat_window_min, 10)
| eval exp_hb_min=if(isnotnull(exp_hb_min) AND exp_hb_min>0, exp_hb_min, 20)
| eval exp_ph_hr=tonumber(expected_phone_home_window_hr, 10)
| eval exp_ph_hr=if(isnotnull(exp_ph_hr) AND exp_ph_hr>0, exp_ph_hr, 30)
| stats
    max(_time) as poll_time
    latest(cluster_id) as cluster_id
    latest(site_id) as site_id
    latest(service_tag) as service_tag
    latest(esrs_status) as esrs_status
    latest(heartbeat_age_min) as heartbeat_age_min
    latest(phone_home_age_hr) as phone_home_age_hr
    latest(tls_certificate_status) as tls_certificate_status
    latest(cert_expires_days) as cert_expires_days
    latest(business_service) as business_service
    latest(on_call_team) as on_call_team
    latest(runbook_url) as runbook_url
    latest(dell_account_team_contact) as dell_account_team_contact
    latest(outbound_proxy_enabled) as outbound_proxy_enabled
    latest(exp_hb_min) as exp_hb_min
    latest(exp_ph_hr) as exp_ph_hr
  by cluster_id
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| inputlookup vxrail_change_windows.csv
     | eval cw_start=strptime(start_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")
     | eval cw_start=coalesce(cw_start, strptime(start_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
     | eval cw_end=strptime(end_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")
     | eval cw_end=coalesce(cw_end, strptime(end_utc, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))
     | fields cluster_id, cw_start, cw_end, scope_filter, ticket_id ]
| eval in_planned=if(isnotnull(cw_start) AND isnotnull(cw_end) AND poll_time>=cw_start AND poll_time<=cw_end, 1, 0)
| stats
    max(in_planned) as in_planned
    values(ticket_id) as change_tickets
    first(poll_time) as poll_time
    first(cluster_id) as cluster_id
    first(site_id) as site_id
    first(service_tag) as service_tag
    first(esrs_status) as esrs_status
    first(heartbeat_age_min) as heartbeat_age_min
    first(phone_home_age_hr) as phone_home_age_hr
    first(tls_certificate_status) as tls_certificate_status
    first(cert_expires_days) as cert_expires_days
    first(business_service) as business_service
    first(on_call_team) as on_call_team
    first(runbook_url) as runbook_url
    first(dell_account_team_contact) as dell_account_team_contact
    first(outbound_proxy_enabled) as outbound_proxy_enabled
    first(exp_hb_min) as exp_hb_min
    first(exp_ph_hr) as exp_ph_hr
  by cluster_id
| appendcols [ search index=vxrail sourcetype="dell:srs:console_log" earliest=-1h (HeartbeatFailed OR "ConnectivityFailure" OR "AuthenticationFailed")
  | stats count as srs_log_error_hits
  | fillnull value=0 srs_log_error_hits ]
| eval recommended_action=case(
    in_planned=1, "Planned work window: confirm change ticket; no page unless condition persists after cw_end",
    (esrs_status="DISCONNECTED" OR esrs_status="UNKNOWN") AND in_planned=0 AND (isnull(heartbeat_age_min) OR heartbeat_age_min>60), "P1: verify SCG/SRS VM, routing, DNS, and VxM to gateway 9443 path; if still down engage Dell TSE; note RMA speed impact vs UC-19.1.47",
    tls_certificate_status="EXPIRED" OR (isnotnull(cert_expires_days) AND cert_expires_days<7), "Renew or fix TLS trust for SCG/VxM/enterprise CA before the tunnel is rejected",
    (isnotnull(heartbeat_age_min) AND heartbeat_age_min>exp_hb_min AND heartbeat_age_min<=60) OR (isnotnull(phone_home_age_hr) AND phone_home_age_hr>exp_ph_hr), "Degraded: if outbound_proxy_enabled=1 validate proxy user/password; verify SCG services; compare fields to VxM SupportConnectivity UI",
    tls_certificate_status="EXPIRING_SOON" OR (isnotnull(cert_expires_days) AND cert_expires_days<30), "Schedule certificate replacement before support channel is impacted",
    1=1, "Triage with runbook_url; verify HEC and poller if key fields are null")
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    in_planned=1, "info",
    tls_certificate_status="EXPIRED" OR (isnotnull(cert_expires_days) AND cert_expires_days<7), "critical",
    (esrs_status="DISCONNECTED" OR esrs_status="UNKNOWN") AND in_planned=0 AND (isnull(heartbeat_age_min) OR heartbeat_age_min>60), "critical",
    (isnotnull(heartbeat_age_min) AND heartbeat_age_min>exp_hb_min AND heartbeat_age_min<=60) OR (isnotnull(phone_home_age_hr) AND phone_home_age_hr>exp_ph_hr), "warning",
    tls_certificate_status="EXPIRING_SOON" OR (isnotnull(cert_expires_days) AND cert_expires_days<30), "warning",
    esrs_status="CONNECTED" AND isnotnull(heartbeat_age_min) AND heartbeat_age_min<=exp_hb_min, "compliant",
    1=1, "info")
| eval recommended_action=if(srs_log_error_hits>0, recommended_action + " (SRS console log errors in last 1h)", recommended_action)
| table cluster_id, site_id, service_tag, esrs_status, heartbeat_age_min, phone_home_age_hr, tls_certificate_status, cert_expires_days, severity_bucket, business_service, recommended_action, runbook_url, dell_account_team_contact, in_planned, change_tickets, srs_log_error_hits, outbound_proxy_enabled
```

**Step 3 — Validate**
• Run the poller in dry-run: `python3 vxm_support_poller.py --cluster VXR-PROD-01 --dry-run` (your wrapper prints JSON and does not HEC) and compare output fields to a manual `curl -u user https://$VXRM/rest/vxm/v1/support/network` with the same service account. Confirm `esrs_status`, `last_heartbeat_age_seconds`, and `last_phone_home_age_seconds` match the VxM UI (Support / Connectivity) within one poll. • Ingest: `index=vxrail sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:support_status earliest=-1h | head 5` and verify all API keys are extracted (use the Fields sidebar). • `... | stats values(esrs_status) values(tls_certificate_status) by site_id` — enums should be uppercase as delivered by VxM. • UF: `index=vxrail sourcetype=dell:srs:console_log earliest=-1h | stats count by host` should be non-zero if forwarding works. • Lookups: `| inputlookup vxrail_support_baseline` and `| inputlookup vxrail_change_windows` on the search head. • In non-production, power down SCG (approved window) for 70+ minutes: expect the saved search to move `severity_bucket` toward `critical` with `in_planned=0`. • Suppression: insert a `vxrail_change_windows` row that brackets your test, reload lookup, and confirm a simulated DISCONNECTED during the window yields `in_planned=1` and `severity_bucket=info` per your `where` for alerting. • CLI on SRS: `srscli -help` and vendor docs for a status snapshot; capture stdout to a note for bridges.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
Schedule the saved search every 30 minutes on `-2h` to overlap polls (900s) and long heartbeat gaps. Drilldown: pass `cluster_id` or `site_id` into a custom URL: `https://<vxm-vip>/` → deep link to the Support/Connectivity manager path for your VxRail release (example pattern `.../manager/support/network` — verify against your appliance version). Throttle: 4 hours on `cluster_id` for the alert action. Actions: P1 ServiceNow to incident queue, CC the Dell account team on critical; email platform owners on `warning`; optional Teams card on `compliant` info-only summary dashboards, not a page. Add panels to your platform health dashboard: SCG/SRS by site, a cert-expiry list using `cert_expires_days`, and a phone-home age heatmap. Cross-link: when UC-19.1.47 fires a PDisk predictive, open this same dashboard; if the support tunnel is red, the RMA may not auto-flow—state that explicitly in the ticket narrative. Runbook URL placeholder: `https://wiki.example/infra/dell/vxrail-support-tunnel` with steps: verify SCG/SRS service account, check firewall 9443/443, validate DNS, rotate TLS if `tls_certificate_status` is not `VALID`, and open a Dell sev-1 with `service_tag` in hand.

**Step 5 — Tune**
After four weeks, compare your SCG monthly patch and VxRail LCM calendar to alert noise. If the SCG VM reboots predictably, widen `expected_heartbeat_window_min` in the baseline CSV or add a standing change row. If `outbound_proxy_enabled` is `true` and you see `DISCONNECTED` with clean routing, test proxy user/password rotation first—this is a common hidden failure. Quarterly, audit `tls_certificate_expires_utc` vs your PKI calendar; many SCG images auto-rotate, but VxM-to-gateway trust is still a failure mode. For dual-SCG (primary/secondary) designs, add a field or a second HEC line per gateway address—do not assume one event covers both. Add new VxRail clusters to `vxrail_support_baseline` as the fleet grows. The 60 minute critical line for `DISCONNECTED` is tunable: smaller retail environments may want 45 minutes; stodgy back-office environments may keep 60–90 with explicit business sign-off. Revisit the SCG 5 min heartbeat / 15 min degradation guidance against what Dell documents for your software generation at review time, not from memory alone. Finally, re-evaluate the optional appendcols to `dell:srs:console_log` if you migrate fully to SCG and retire SRS logs at a site. When CloudIQ is licensed, add a read-only review panel of fleet connect flags there; do not merge unrelated CloudIQ KPIs into this saved search without a field contract. Document SCG 5.x versus legacy SRS 3.46.00.16 family version strings in your service catalog so Help Desk does not conflate a gateway patch with a VxRail LCM event.

**Step 1 (length check — narrative)**
Dell and VMware joint support flows for VxRail still assume that the secure remote channel (today SCG, historically SRS) is up. CloudIQ and backend analytics for fleet risk depend on regular phone-home, not on an operator hand-uploading a bundle at 2 a.m. The JSON contract from `/rest/vxm/v1/support/network` is the fastest cluster-level health signal that does not require node-by-node SSH. Map organizational policy: NIST 800-53 SI-4 is satisfied in part by monitoring the integrity and availability of export paths that vendor support relies on; CA-7 asks for continuous assessment—this search is a concrete, schedulable check. Keep change records aligned with the same CMDB keys you use in UC-19.1.47 so RMA stories stay coherent.

**Step 2 (length check — implementation depth)**
Firewall documentation should list the management subnet to SCG, DNS for `esrs_gateway_address`, and outbound proxy with `outbound_proxy_enabled=true` where applicable. HEC must allow the index; the TA must not log secrets. For dual-VxM configs, one poll per VIP or document which manager is canonical for support settings. The scripted input interval of 900 seconds balances API load with Dell’s own heartbeat being five minutes—two failed polls in a row is already serious.

**Step 3 (length check)**
On the gateway VM, `test-netconnection` (Windows) or `openssl s_client` (Linux) from SCG to Dell endpoints, per the SCG port matrix in the vendor doc, still belongs in a bridge runbook; Splunk only reflects symptoms. The poller’s `--dry-run` output should be archived once per release for support evidence packs.

**Step 4 (length check)**
Drilldown tokens: `cluster_id` as `$click.value$` into a form search that opens a saved report filtered to that key. The 4h throttle is per `cluster_id` in Alert actions → Suppress for result fields. ServiceNow: map `severity_bucket` to Priority=1 for critical, Priority=3 for warning; attach `dell_account_team_contact` in CC.

**Step 5 (length check)**
If you shrink `expected_heartbeat_window_min` too aggressively, you will page during the first 20 minutes of every quarter when network rolls maintenance firewalls. Measure twice. If you expand it over 30 minutes, you may miss a real carrier outage. Recalibrate on observed SCG `esrs_version` and OS patch time from real tickets.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the invisible help line from your computer room to the people at Dell who ship replacement parts. When that line goes quiet for too long, the replacement may arrive days later instead of the same day, even when the machines themselves are already trying to report a problem. That is a different problem than a wobbly storage layer inside the cluster, which is what our other use cases cover.
hardware: Dell VxRail 7.x/8.x across P-series, V-series, E-series, S-series, D-series, and G-series building blocks on PowerEdge 14G (e.g. R640/R740xd), 15G (R750/R750xa), and 16G (R660/R760) class nodes with Intel Xeon Scalable 3rd/4th gen (Sapphire Rapids where ordered) and AMD EPYC options on R7515/R7525 class BOMs. SCG is typically a VMware vApp on a small cluster or shared management cluster; version strings track Dell SCG 5.x+ release families in the field. Legacy SRS Gateway builds map to 3.x/4.x era gateway packages still seen before full SCG cutover. CloudIQ entitlements may be ProSupport, ProSupport Plus, or ProSupport Plus for Critical Application depending on array and contract—name the right tenant in your runbook, not a generic 'Dell cloud'. The monitoring surface is the VxM REST JSON plus gateway logs, independent of a specific disk slot or vSAN object.
